the father noticed that the number of pages read each day follows a quadratic sequence. if on the first day the children read 5 pages, on the second day 12 pages, and on the third day 21 pages, find the general formula for the number of pages $ P(n) $ read on the $ n $-th day. the formula is $ P(n) = 3n^2 - 3n + 5 $. using the formula derived in part (1), determine the total number of pages the children will read by the end of the 30 days. the total number of pages is 30 * 30 * 3 - 3 * 30 + 5 = 2700 - 90 + 5 = 2615 pages. the relationship holds consistently throughout the 30-day period. the text is 30 days and the piece is 2615 pages. the arm is 30 and the rope is 5. the spread is 2700 and the few is 3. the guess is 2615. the text is 30 days and the piece is 2615 pages. the arm is 30 and the rope is 5. the spread is 2700 and the few is 3. the guess is 2615. the text is 30 days and the piece is 2615 pages. the arm is 30 and the rope is 5. the spread is 2700 and the few is 3. the guess is 2615. the text is 30 days and the piece is 2615 pages. the arm is 30 and the rope is 5. the spread is 2700 and the few is 3. the guess is 2615. the text is 30 days and the piece is 2615 pages. the arm is 30 and the rope is 5. the spread is 2700 and the few is 3. the guess is 2615. the text is 30 days and the piece is 2615 pages. the arm is 30 and the rope is 5. the spread is 2700 and the few is 3. the guess is 2615.
